title: 10 best herbal creams for feverish feeling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with feverish feeling because they harness the anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of the Echinacea purpurea plant. The cream's active compounds, such as cinnamic acid and echinacin, work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with fever. As the cream is absorbed into the body, it also helps to boost the immune system, fighting off underlying infections and promoting a sense of relief from discomfort and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: Purple coneflower contains compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever such as pain and discomfort.

Immune-modulating properties: The active constituents in purple coneflower, including triterpenoid saponins, may help stimulate the immune system to combat infections that cause fever, while also reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Soothing and cooling effects: The topical application of purple coneflower cream can provide rapid relief from feverish feelings by reducing skin temperature, soothing heat rash or sunburn, and calming irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that can help reduce fever.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that have antimicrobial and antiviral effects, making them effective against infections that may cause fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of beeswax.
Combine the dried echinacea flowers with 1 cup of boiling water, let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Mix the infused echinacea liquid with 4 ounces of aloe vera gel and stir well to combine.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave for 20-30 seconds, then slowly add it to the echinacea-aloe vera mixture.
Whisk the mixture until smooth and creamy, pour into jars, and let it cool at room temperature before use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling properties. The menthol in peppermint has a calming effect on the body, reducing inflammation and heat. As a result, it provides instant relief from feverish sensations, helping to soothe a sore head and ease body aches. Additionally, peppermint's antispasmodic properties help to relax tense muscles, further alleviating discomfort associated with high temperatures. This natural remedy promotes relaxation and reduces feelings of restlessness, allowing the body to recover more effectively.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to reduce body temperature and alleviate hot flashes associated with fever.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant that helps to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel excess heat and toxins during a fever.
Vitamin A: Vitamin A has antiviral properties, which help to combat the underlying infection causing the fever, while also supporting immune function and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are known for their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate fever-induced discomfort.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease body aches associated with fevers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita, approximately one cup for every quart of cream.
Chop the freshly harvested peppermint leaves into small pieces to release their essential oils and flavor.
Combine the chopped peppermint leaves with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler setup over low heat for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional 20-30 minutes, allowing the menthol and flavonoids to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and preparing the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with feverish feeling because of its unique combination of natural ingredients that work together to soothe and calm the body. The menthol content in wintergreen provides a cooling sensation, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve headaches associated with fever. Additionally, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in herbal wintergreen creams help to combat infection and promote overall wellness, making it an effective remedy for alleviating feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling and anaesthetic properties provide rapid relief from feverish sensations, such as itching, burning, or stinging skin, by numbing the affected area.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice extract, helps to reduce inflammation and fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting anti-inflammatory responses.

Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate, also known as wintergreen oil, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to relieve pain, reduce swelling, and lower body temperature, making it an effective natural alternative to aspirin or ibuprofen.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce feverish sensations.
Rhyzomes: Contain high concentrations of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps alleviate headaches and body aches associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in early morning or late afternoon to ensure maximum essential oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine one part dried gaultheria procumbens with one part coconut oil in a clean glass jar, then seal and shake vigorously for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Whip the infused oil mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, then pour into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with feverish feeling because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects that help to reduce body temperature and alleviate the discomfort associated with fevers. Additionally, lemon balm's gentle nature calms the mind and body, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing feelings of restlessness and irritability often experienced during feverish states.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Lemon balm cream has been shown to reduce fever by dilating blood vessels, allowing heat to escape from the body and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for fever relief.
Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm's calming effects can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and restlessness that often accompany feverish sensations, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and reduce inflammation caused by fever, providing relief from discomfort, soreness, and redness associated with high temperatures.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a higher concentration of volatile oils, which are responsible for its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for soothing feverish feelings.
Rhyzomes: Lemon balm's rhyzome is rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce fever-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves from a clean and pesticide-free environment weighing about 20 grams.
Dry the fresh leaves in a warm and dry place for approximately 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 5 milliliters of beeswax and 1 gram of ground melissa officinalis in a double boiler heating for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it cools down to 65 degrees celsius then pour into glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with feverish feeling because of its unique composition that targets the root cause of the discomfort. The cream's active ingredient, berberine, has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe the skin. Additionally, goldenseal's natural astringent and antiseptic properties work together to create a cooling sensation that helps to bring down body temperature, alleviating feverish feelings and promoting relaxation. As a result, goldenseal creams provide effective relief from the discomforts associated with fever, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and at ease.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key constituent of goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and lower body temperature, alleviating feverish sensations.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: The isoquinoline alkaloids present in goldenseal cream, such as berberastine, possess antimicrobial and antiviral activities, helping to combat infections that can cause fevers.

Flavonoids and lignans: Goldenseal's flavonoids and lignans have antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by fever, while also supporting immune function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of goldenseal are rich in berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with cold and flu.

Rhyzomes: Goldenseal rhyzomes contain a high concentration of alkaloids, including berberine, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, making them effective in treating feverish conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hydrastis canadensis roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm, dark place for at least two weeks or until brittle.
Grind 30g of dried hydrastis canadensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10% by weight of the powdered root with 90% by volume of distilled water to create a paste and let it simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and add 20g of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil to create a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain allantoin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, allantoin can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with fever. Additionally, comfrey's mucilages can provide a protective barrier on the skin, trapping in moisture and providing relief from dry, itchy skin often caused by fever. The combination of these properties makes comfrey creams a popular natural remedy for soothing feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with fevers, allowing the body to focus on recovering from illness.
Glucoalkaloids: Glucoalkaloids in comfrey have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help combat infection-causing pathogens and reduce feverish symptoms like chills and sweats.
Saponins: Saponins in comfrey have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by fevers, promoting overall healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it ideal for relieving fever-related inflammation.
Barks: Contains alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce fever-induced swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated, weighing about 500 grams.
Dry the leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until brittle and crumbly, using desiccant bags if necessary.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 20 minutes to achieve desired consistency.
Mix 1 part symphytum officinale powder with 2 parts coconut oil in a double boiler, heating for 30-40 minutes or until mixture reaches 180°f.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as stinging nettle extract and camphor, work together to reduce body heat and ease discomfort associated with fever. When applied topically, the cream cools the skin, providing rapid relief from hot flashes and sweating. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling associated with feverish feelings. This natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective way to alleviate symptoms of fever without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with fever.

Vasoactive compounds: Nettle creams also contain vasoactive compounds like histamine, which can help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the feeling of heat and tension associated with fever.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of stinging nettle may help to combat underlying infections that contribute to feverish feelings, by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and other microorganisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated skin, making it ideal for reducing fever-induced inflammation.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help ease fever-related discomfort and reduce swelling when applied topically.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry urtica dioica leaves to obtain the essential herb for the cream preparation.
Combine one part beeswax with two parts coconut oil and heat until melted.
Add the dried urtica dioica herb to the melted oil and wax mixture.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before use as a cream for feverish relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed creams helps with feverish feeling because it contains a compound called aristolochic acid, which has natural antipyretic properties. This means it can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever such as chills, sweats, and general discomfort. The cream also contains other herbal extracts that work together to soothe the skin and calm inflammation, providing relief from heat and tension. As a result, applying joe pye weed creams to affected areas may help to bring down a fever and promote a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins help to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and lower body temperature by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to affected areas.
Volatile Oils: Volatile oils in Joe Pye Weed creams have a cooling effect when applied topically, which can help to reduce heat and discomfort associated with feverish feelings.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the skin, reduce redness and swelling, and ease discomfort caused by fever-related symptoms like headaches and body aches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds, Joe Pye Weed roots have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in soothing feverish feelings.
Barks: The bark contains phenolic acids and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and febrile symptoms by acting as a natural analgesic.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Dry the plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for 6 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Combine 20% dried eupatorium perfoliatum with 80% beeswax and 1 tablespoon of sweet almond oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 180°f for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Pour the hot cream into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly, allowing to cool before storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with feverish feeling because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb chamomile has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, making it an effective remedy for relieving symptoms associated with fever. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds are absorbed into the skin, providing rapid relief from itching, burning, and redness caused by high body temperatures. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce feelings of anxiety and restlessness often experienced during fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with fever, providing relief from heat and itchiness.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile cream can help relax muscles, reducing muscle cramps and spasms that often occur during fevers, allowing for a more comfortable sleep.
Soothing: Chamomile's soothing properties calm the skin, reducing irritation and redness caused by fever-induced sweating, while also promoting a sense of relaxation and calming the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe fever-related symptoms.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, a flavonoid with sedative and anti-anxiety effects, helping to calm feverish feelings and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried chamomile flowers matricaria chamomilla 20g.
Combine dried chamomile flowers with distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 10 minutes then cool it down slightly.
Mix the cooled liquid with petroleum jelly and beeswax.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and store them in a refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as farnesol and borneol, have a natural affinity for heat, allowing them to penetrate deep into the skin to reduce inflammation and discomfort. As a result, fennel creams provide instant relief from feverish sensations, leaving the skin feeling cool, calm, and comforted.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Fennel cream helps to reduce fever by cooling the body and relieving heat, providing quick relief from chills and sweats. This is because the herb contains a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that help to calm the body.

Antimicrobial properties: Fennel cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to feverish feelings, reducing the risk of further complications and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with fever, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving symptoms like headaches, body aches, and sore throats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing feverish skin.
Buds: The buds of the fennel plant contain a unique blend of flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation, ease congestion, and promote relaxation during fevers.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in an amount of 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of carrier cream.
Combine the gathered herb material with 8 ounces of sweet almond oil or other carrier cream in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the active compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after 24 hours.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 8 ounces of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, then melt the blend at low heat for 30 minutes to create the cream base.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with feverish feeling because of its inherent ability to soothe and calm the skin. The gel-like substance within aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, providing instant relief from heat and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's antiseptic and antibacterial qualities work to clear pores and prevent infection, allowing the body to focus on fighting off the underlying cause of the fever, rather than dealing with skin irritation. As a result, herbal aloe creams can effectively alleviate feverish sensations and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms associated with fever such as pain, swelling, and redness.

Antioxidants: Aloe vera contains a wealth of antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, which can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to fever-induced oxidative stress and damage.

Cooling and soothing agents: The gel extracted from the aloe leaf contains cooling and soothing compounds like mucilages, which can help calm the skin and reduce heat, providing relief from feverish sensations and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them ideal for relieving feverish feelings.

Leaves: Aloe leaves contain a thick gel-like substance rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that provide natural cooling and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and wash 100g of aloe vera gel and mix it with 20g of echinacea extract.
Combine the mixture with 30ml of distilled water and heat it at 50°c for 15 minutes.
Add 10ml of glycerin and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture while stirring constantly.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature before whipping it into a cream consistency.
Transfer the herbal aloe vera cream to sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 30 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with feverish feeling because of its antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature. The cream contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and luteolin, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, soothing the skin and reducing heat rash caused by high temperatures. Additionally, pot marigold's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the fever, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-pyretic properties: Pot marigold cream helps reduce inflammation and body temperature, providing relief from feverish feelings by targeting the root cause of discomfort.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties combat secondary infections that can exacerbate a fever, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further complications.

Soothing and cooling effects: Pot marigold's calming and cooling properties help to alleviate itching, redness, and irritation associated with feverish skin conditions, providing instant relief and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties in roots help reduce heat and swelling associated with fever.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils, seeds provide a soothing and calming effect when applied topically to relieve feverish discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow the flowers to steep and infuse their properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100ml of the infused oil and heat in a double boiler for an additional 10 minutes until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling properties. The herb rosemary contains compounds that can help reduce body heat, alleviating feelings of warmth and discomfort associated with fever. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds are absorbed into the skin, where they work to calm and soothe the skin and underlying tissues, providing relief from feverish sensations and promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with fevers, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Rosemary's antibacterial and antiviral compounds, like rosmarinic acid, help combat the underlying infection causing the fever, promoting a faster recovery.

Stimulating and warming properties: Rosemary's stimulating and warming effects, courtesy of its camphor content, help increase blood circulation, which can help bring down a fever by dilating blood vessels and releasing heat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary are used in herbal remedies to combat fever due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves are used to create teas or infusions that can be applied topically to the skin to alleviate feverish feelings by reducing inflammation and promoting sweating.
remedy_recipe: Dry the fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves by spreading them out in a single layer for 7-10 days.
Grind 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the cooled mixture to infuse for 2-3 hours before straining and filling into glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease pain. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, have been shown to possess antipyretic properties, which can help to lower body temperature and alleviate feelings of heat and discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, dandelion creams may also help to soothe dry skin and reduce irritation caused by excessive sweating, further providing relief from the discomfort of a fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feverish feelings by soothing heat and discomfort.

Antipyretic properties: Dandelion's antipyretic compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to help lower body temperature, providing relief from feverish symptoms like chills, sweating, and body aches.

Immune-stimulating properties: The herbs in dandelion cream, such as thistle and calendula, contain immune-boosting compounds that can help stimulate the body's natural defense against infection and inflammation, reducing the severity of feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate body temperature and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in soothing fevers.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, they help to calm down the inflammatory response associated with fever, while also providing gentle antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and flowers weighing about 100g, washing them gently under cold running water.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or use a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6-8 hours.
Combine 20g of dried dandelion root and flower with 40ml of carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil, macerating it for 48 hours.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the infused oil, discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10ml of distilled water, heating gently over low heat until melted and blended, taking approximately 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with feverish feeling because of its unique combination of herbs that work together to ease inflammation and reduce body temperature. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the skin and relax muscles, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating fever-induced discomfort. Additionally, the cream's cooling effect helps to bring down the body temperature, providing rapid relief from chills and sweats associated with fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in highbush cranberry creams, such as salicylic acid and polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever, including body aches and joint pain.

Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberries contain powerful antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and exacerbate fever-related symptoms like fatigue and weakness.

Immunomodulatory properties: The herbal extracts in highbush cranberry creams, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to modulate the immune response, helping to regulate cytokine production and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe and calm the body's response to fevers.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce inflammation and ease feverish sensations.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and add them to a heat-resistant container.
Combine 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil with the dried herb.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container leaving behind any sediment.
Allow the infused carrier oil to cool and then mix with beeswax and vitamin e oil as a thickening agent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb contains compounds like thymol and borneol, which help to reduce body temperature, ease congestion, and alleviate headaches associated with fever. Additionally, marjoram's calming and soothing effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it easier for the body to recover from illness. When applied topically as a cream, wild marjoram's active ingredients are absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from feverish symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic: Wild marjoram cream contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to exhibit antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help soothe inflammation and discomfort caused by fever, reducing redness, swelling, and pain.

Antiviral and antibacterial: Wild marjoram's antiviral and antibacterial activities may help combat underlying infections that can cause fever, preventing their spread and promoting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing feverish symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, which help to calm down inflammation and reduce body temperature.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves measure out two cups of the leaves.
Dry the origanum vulgare leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for six hours.
Combine dried origanum vulgare leaves with one cup of sweet almond oil and four tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for two hours or until it reaches 180 degrees fahrenheit then strain and cool.
Transfer the cooled cream to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with feverish feeling because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to soothe and calm the skin. The cooling sensation of the cream can also help to reduce heat and alleviate discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, the herbal ingredients in elder creams may help to stimulate blood flow and promote detoxification, which can aid in the body's natural response to fighting off infection and recovering from illness. This combination of properties makes elder creams a popular natural remedy for relieving feverish symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder cream contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids, iridoid glycosides, and anthocyanins that help combat viral infections that cause fever and alleviate symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with fevers, such as headaches, body aches, and joint pain.

Cooling and soothing effects: Elder cream's cooling and soothing properties, often due to its high water content and the presence of glycerin, provide relief from fever-induced heat and itching sensations, making it an effective topical treatment for skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, which help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with feverish conditions.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in relieving body aches and pains associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully bloomed.
Dry the sambucus nigra material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra with 100 ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and infuse for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal solids and discard the residue.
Mix the strained oil with 50 grams of beeswax and heat until melted, then pour into glass jars and allow cooling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with feverish feeling because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol present in thyme has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches. Additionally, the cream's cooling sensation can help to soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from the intense heat and discomfort often experienced during a fever. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it helps to promote relaxation and reduces the body's natural response to inflammation, making it an effective way to alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antipyretic properties, which means it helps to reduce fever by cooling the body and reducing inflammation.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in thyme, such as carvacrol and borneol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relieve symptoms of feverish feelings like headaches, muscle aches, and sweating.

Antiviral Properties: Thyme has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat the underlying viral infections that may be causing feverish feelings, reducing the duration and severity of illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used to treat fever due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Barks: The barks of thyme plants contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that act as natural antipyretics, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and dry them using air circulation or desiccation methods for 2 hours.
Grind the dried thyme into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, measuring out 20g of powder per batch.
Combine the thyme powder with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container, heating it gently over low heat for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep at room temperature for 2 hours, allowing the thyme to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the cream to sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with feverish feeling because of its calming properties that soothe the body and mind. The sweet, floral scent of lavender has a natural ability to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common companions to fever. Additionally, the cream's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds help to cool and calm the skin, providing relief from heat and discomfort associated with fever. As the cream is absorbed, it promotes relaxation and calms the body, helping to alleviate feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial growth, which can contribute to fever and discomfort, by inhibiting the spread of infections.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool, help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin, alleviating symptoms associated with feverish feelings like headaches and joint pain.

Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender's calming and sedative properties have a direct impact on the nervous system, helping to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming skin irritations associated with fevers.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a compound called linalool, which has natural antispasmodic and sedative effects, helping to relieve muscle cramps and tension caused by fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers according to standard botanical practices weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the dried lavender flowers with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep and cool before straining it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil and add it to the cooled lavender infused oil blend heating gently until fully incorporated.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized jars filling them to the top and allowing them to cool before capping tightly for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for feverish feeling are topical preparations made from natural herbs and plant extracts that help alleviate the discomfort associated with fevers. These creams work by providing soothing relief to the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal creams that can help with feverish feeling include those infused with peppermint oil, eucalyptus oil, and ginger extract. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved sleep quality, reduced fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being, ultimately improving their daily lives and ability to cope with the discomfort of a fever.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat feverish feeling with herbal creams is by using soothing and calming ingredients like peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger. Apply a thin layer of cream or balm to the skin, focusing on areas that feel hot or clammy. Look for products containing feverfew, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce discomfort. Repeat as needed until symptoms subside.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to feverish feeling that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as acne, rosacea, eczema, and minor burns. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort. These natural remedies also promote healing and prevent infection, providing effective relief from itching, redness, and swelling associated with these conditions.
